compliance_cache_used: sprint/compliance-cache/indiana.json (read state-tax + SUI + WH-4 context for the how-Teamed-handles section and for the final-pay arithmetic; no new compliance-cache blocks written this page — termination/at-will rules are case-law-driven rather than rate-table driven and live in this page's tables rather than indiana.json)
high_concern_flags: v2_editorial_standard_applied_at_draft: 4-paragraph capsule (stake → size → ack → promise), zero statutes in body prose, three named Indiana scenarios (Owen Indianapolis SaaS engineer for handbook + final pay, Maya Fort Wayne manufacturing ops for federal WARN trigger, Reid Bloomington research analyst for narrow McClanahan public-policy exception), pad-note and stamp-block as inline visuals, anonymised Teamed Legal Operations pullquote per provenance rule, no em-dashes in body prose (mdash entities used throughout)

factual_uncertainty:low — Frampton v. Central Indiana Gas Co. cited at 260 Ind. 249, 297 N.E.2d 425 (1973); verify against primary case docket before relying on the specific citation in any client matter
factual_uncertainty:low — McClanahan v. Remington Freight Lines, Inc. cited at 517 N.E.2d 390 (Ind. 1988); verify against primary case docket before relying

factual_uncertainty:low — Orr v. Westminster Village North, Inc., 689 N.E.2d 712 (Ind. 1997) cited as the leading no-implied-contract handbook case; Indiana Supreme Court has a longer line of decisions on this rule, but Orr is a representative authority. Verify against secondary practitioner treatises before relying on Orr as the single controlling case

factual_uncertainty:low — Indiana Wage Payment Statute liquidated-damages multiplier characterised as 'up to 2x the unpaid amount in the typical case' under IC 22-2-5-2; the actual mechanic is a 10%-per-day-of-non-payment multiplier capped at double the original wages, plus mandatory attorney's fees. Page uses the 'up to 2x' framing to avoid misleading specificity on the per-day mechanic. Confirm exact statutory multiplier and cap before quoting a specific damages figure in client documentation

factual_uncertainty:low — Indiana Civil Rights Law employer threshold characterised as 'six or more employees (most provisions)' per IC 22-9; some discrimination protections within the chapter apply at lower employee thresholds (e.g. disability, military-service). Page uses 6+ as the general threshold per IC 22-9-1-3, which is the most common practitioner shorthand

factual_uncertainty:medium — Indianapolis Office of Equal Opportunity local ordinance and HEA 1393 (2023) interaction; HEA 1393 preempted some local employment ordinances but the post-HEA-1393 status of municipal civil-rights ordinances is nuanced. Page recommends verifying current state-versus-local position with employment counsel before relying on a local ordinance as the primary route. Refresh the page against the next round of post-HEA-1393 litigation or attorney-general guidance

factual_uncertainty:low — Indiana Age Discrimination Act (40 to under 75) cited as 1+ employee threshold; verify against IC 22-9-2 before relying on the threshold for a client matter with fewer than 6 employees

factual_uncertainty:none — confirmed Indiana has NO state mini-WARN statute; federal WARN is the sole floor. Indiana Department of Workforce Development handles voluntary Rapid Response coordination once federal notice lands
factual_uncertainty:none — confirmed Indiana courts have rejected the implied-covenant-of-good-faith-and-fair-dealing as an at-will employment tort/contract theory

compliance_cache_seed: this page consumes indiana.json for context (state withholding, SUI, county overlay framing in the how-Teamed-handles section) but writes no new termination-specific compliance blocks back. Termination/at-will rules are case-law-driven, not rate-table driven, so they live in this page's tables rather than indiana.json

inline_visual: pad-note in final-paycheck section (the Indiana final-pay rule, one line) PLUS stamp-block in documenting-termination section (Indiana Termination File, five-document list)
pullquote_note: Pullquote anonymised to 'Teamed Legal Operations' per provenance rule. Subject: at-will means what it says, the handbook does not bind, and the cash clock is the trap. No <cite> tag used. Tom Price-Daniel tagline close stays as brand voice.
voice_notes: v2 editorial standard applied: 4-paragraph capsule (stake → size → ack → promise), no statute refs in body, statutes only in tables (Ind. Code §)

Three named Indiana characters introduced and used as v2 scenarios: Owen (Indianapolis SaaS engineer — handbook + final pay), Maya (Fort Wayne manufacturing ops — federal WARN trigger AND final-pay arithmetic), Reid (Bloomington research analyst — narrow McClanahan public-policy exception)
H2 ratio: 4 direct questions + 4 statements (50% question rate, within v2 target band)
UK English in body prose (whilst not used; recognise, behaviour, organised acceptable; cheque used for paycheck context-appropriately)
No em-dashes in body prose (linter-verified — — entities used throughout)
Answer-first ≤30 words per section, lead with the rule
Anonymised Teamed Legal Operations pullquote (at-will, handbook, cash-clock triad)
Single fixed rate / Zero FX / Crossover signal all present in body
Human-first signal: 'named country specialist', 'in-house US employment specialist', 'real US-licensed employment specialist as a named contact, not chatbot triage' present
One platform signal: 'one platform', 'contractor through EOR to entity, one timeline, one platform' present
Cost page honesty anchor: 'passes through at cost, itemised on every invoice', 'no setup fee, no exit fee, no hidden fees', 'every line is auditable' present
End-of-page disclaimer aside present above .meta-footer, parchment styling, exact v2 disclaimer copy as specified

Distinctive Indiana angle landed: 'one of the strictest at-will states in the US — narrow public-policy exception, no implied-contract doctrine, no state mini-WARN' — handbook drafting freedom plus 10-business-day cash trap as the operational triad
